sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Vitamin D: supplement use in specific population groups
sources.version: NICE public health guideline PH56; last updated 30 August 2017
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom public-health guidance; UAE thresholds, exposure and treatment pathways require adaptation
sources.population: People at risk of low vitamin D status, with specific pregnancy, breastfeeding, age, skin pigmentation, sun-exposure and clinical-risk qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Public health and selected primary-care testing and supplement-use review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for risk and qualified testing-context documentation only. It does not support automatic deficiency interpretation, dose, regimen, treatment duration or UAE laboratory thresholds.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1–6 and glossary — specific at-risk populations
exact_sections.locator: official recommendations and linked definitions of specific population groups
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recording of age, pregnancy or breastfeeding, low sun exposure, covering skin, darker skin and other entered risk context without diagnosing deficiency.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 7 — only test when symptomatic, very high risk, or clinically indicated
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting why an existing vitamin D test was clinically obtained and whether symptoms or high-risk context were assessed; it does not interpret the result or generate testing.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 8 — recording supplement use in specified encounters
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician documentation of already reported vitamin D supplement use in qualified encounters without generating product, dose or duration.
